title: AiProposeArgs
description:
Args for `com.nexus.ai::propose_tool_calls`.

Single-turn entry point that runs the provider once with the
registry advertised, returns the model's `tool_use` blocks
**without executing any of them**, and exits. Unlike
`stream_chat` there is no dispatch loop: the caller is
responsible for invoking the returned tool calls (or not).

Used by `nexus-agent` (ADR 0023) to derive a [`Plan`] from
provider tool-use blocks instead of free-form JSON; preserves
the agent's pre-execution approval flow.

[`Plan`]: nexus_agent::Plan

messages.description:
Conversation messages â€” same shape as `stream_chat`. Most
callers pass a single user turn carrying the goal. Legacy,
text-only path: superseded by `turns` when that is non-empty.

system.description:
Optional system prompt forwarded to the provider verbatim.
Unlike `stream_chat`, this handler does NOT prepend the host
system-prompt floor â€” agent planning prompts are caller-owned.

tools.description:
Tool-advertisement policy. Omit for the default `auto`. The
policy controls which schemas the model sees; the handler
returns whatever tool calls the model requests.

turns.description:
Phase 5.5 (2c) â€” rich, linkage-preserving conversation turns.
When present and non-empty this **supersedes** `messages`: the
handler builds provider-native [`crate::provider::ChatTurn`]s
directly from it, so assistant `tool_use` blocks and their
`tool_result` replies reach the model intact. Absent / empty
keeps the legacy text-only `messages` path.

AiChatTurn.description:
One conversation turn on the `propose_tool_calls` wire that
preserves the assistant `tool_use` â†” `tool_result` linkage a plain
text-only [`AiStreamAskMessage`] cannot. Phase 5.5 (2c): the agent
loop sends the whole conversation as `turns` so the provider sees a
real multi-turn exchange instead of a restated-goal blob. Mirrors
[`crate::provider::ChatTurn`] across the IPC boundary.

oneOf.description: A user message â€” plain text.
An assistant message that may carry text and/or tool calls.

content.description:
Assistant narration; empty when the model emitted only
tool-use blocks.

tool_calls.description:
Tool calls the model issued, in order. Empty for a
text-only assistant turn.

oneOf.description: The dispatcher's reply to one assistant tool call.

content.description:
Stringified tool result (or the error message when
`is_error`).

is_error.description:
`true` when the tool returned an error; providers surface
this distinctly from a successful result.

AiStreamAskMessage.description:
One message in a chat conversation. Mirror of
[`crate::provider::ChatMessage`].

role.description: The role of the message sender.

AiStreamAskRole.description:
Role of a participant in a chat conversation. Mirror of
[`crate::provider::Role`] for schema emission.

AiToolPolicy.description:
Tool-advertisement policy for `stream_chat`.

`Auto` (default) hands the registry's full schema list to the
provider so the model can call tools. `None` advertises an empty
list â€” used by `mode=complete` and by ghost-completion callers that
must never trigger side effects.

oneOf.description:
Advertise the full registry to the model â€” including
mutating tools (`write_file`). Per ADR 0022 Phase 2 this
requires the caller to hold `ai.tools.write` in addition to
`ai.chat`.
Advertise no tools â€” the model cannot issue tool calls.

`Auto` plus tools dynamically discovered from
`com.nexus.mcp.host::list_servers` / `list_tools`. Each MCP
tool is exposed as `mcp__<server>__<tool>` and proxied to
`call_tool`. Discovery runs once per `stream_chat` call (no
cache); a server that fails to enumerate is skipped with a
warning so the chat call still proceeds. Per ADR 0022
Phase 2 this requires `ai.tools.write` and `ai.tools.mcp`.

`Auto`, restricted to read-only built-ins (`read_file`,
`search_forge`, `list_backlinks`, `git_log`). Surfaces a
productive subset of the registry to callers that hold
`ai.chat` but not `ai.tools.write`. Per ADR 0022 Phase 2.

AiTurnToolCall.description:
One tool call inside an [`AiChatTurn::Assistant`] turn â€” the rich,
linkage-preserving form used by the Phase 5.5 (2c) multi-turn
`propose_tool_calls` path. Mirrors the model's native `tool_use`
block: `id` is the provider-issued handle the matching
[`AiChatTurn::ToolResult`] echoes back so the model can correlate a
result with the call that produced it.

id.description:
Provider-issued tool-use id (e.g. `toolu_â€¦`). The matching
`tool_result` turn echoes this back.

input.description: Decoded tool arguments the model emitted.

name.description: Tool name as advertised in the registry.
